soilPreference: Well-drained, rich loam, slightly acidic (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Indeterminate. Extremely productive; harvest regularly to encourage continued fruiting. Stake or cage for support.

soilPreference: Well-drained, rich loam, slightly acidic (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Indeterminate. Large fruits up to 2 lbs. Requires strong staking or caging. Prune suckers for larger fruit.

soilPreference: Well-drained, rich loam, slightly acidic (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Determinate paste tomato. Heavy producer ideal for sauces and canning. Sets fruit in concentrated window.

soilPreference: Well-drained, rich loam, slightly acidic (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Harvest green or wait for full color (red, yellow, orange) for sweeter flavor. Needs warm soil above 65F.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)
Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Harvest at 6-8 inches for best flavor. Provide trellis to save space and reduce disease. Needs consistent water.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Harvest small (2-4 inches) for best pickles. Very prolific; pick frequently to encourage production.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Long, thin-skinned, seedless type. Best grown on trellis. Harvest at 12-14 inches. Prefers warm protected conditions.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam with consistent moisture (pH 6.0-7.0)
Well-drained, fertile loam with consistent moisture (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Soft, tender leaves with mild flavor. Bolts in heat; grow in spring and fall. Harvest outer leaves or whole head.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam with consistent moisture (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Upright heads with crisp ribs. More heat-tolerant than butterhead. Cut-and-come-again or harvest full heads.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam with consistent moisture (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Crisp, tight heads. Needs cool weather; very bolt-prone in heat. Harvest when heads feel firm.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam with consistent moisture (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Loose-leaf type with red-tinged foliage. Fast-growing and bolt-resistant. Excellent for cut-and-come-again harvest.

soilPreference: Deep, loose, sandy loam free of rocks (pH 6.0-6.8)
Deep, loose, sandy loam free of rocks (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Cylindrical, blunt-tipped roots with sweet flavor. Needs loose, stone-free soil. Keep soil moist until germination (10-14 days).

soilPreference: Deep, loose, sandy loam free of rocks (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Tapered, sturdy roots that tolerate heavier soils. Good storage variety. 6-8 inch roots at maturity.

soilPreference: Deep, loose, sandy loam free of rocks (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Short, broad roots (5-6 inches). Excellent for heavy or shallow soils. Sweet flavor; good for cooking and storage.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)
Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: All-purpose storage onion. Choose long-day or short-day varieties for your latitude. Cure 2-3 weeks after harvest.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Mild, sweet flavor; excellent raw in salads. Shorter storage life than yellow. Harvest when tops fall over naturally.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Sharp, clean flavor. Papery white skin. Shorter storage than yellow varieties. Commonly used in Mexican and white sauces.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam with good organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)
Well-drained, fertile loam with good organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Plant individual cloves in fall. Best for mild climates and long storage. Braidable stems. Harvest when lower leaves brown.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam with good organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Plant cloves in fall; needs cold period. Produces edible scapes in spring. Larger cloves but shorter storage than softneck.

soilPreference: Well-drained, moderately fertile loam (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Compact bush habit; no support needed. Pick frequently when pencil-thin for tender beans. Do not sow in cold wet soil.

soilPreference: Well-drained, moderately fertile loam (pH 6.0-7.5)

scheduling.notes: Edible pods with sweet peas inside. Provide 4-6 ft trellis. Sow as early as soil can be worked. Harvest when pods are plump.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-6.8)
Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in compost (pH 6.0-7.5)
Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in compost (pH 6.0-7.5)

scheduling.notes: Classic dark green zucchini. Harvest at 6-8 inches; check daily as fruit grows fast. Bush habit saves space.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in compost (pH 6.0-7.5)

scheduling.notes: Bright yellow skin. Similar culture to green zucchini. Slightly sweeter flavor. Easy to spot when ready to pick.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)
Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Classic broccoli with large central head. Cut head before yellow flowers open. Side shoots continue producing for weeks.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Italian heirloom producing small central head then many side shoots. Cut-and-come-again habit extends harvest significantly.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam with consistent moisture (pH 6.0-7.0)
Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in organic matter (pH 6.0-7.5)
Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in nitrogen (pH 6.0-7.5)
Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in nitrogen (pH 6.0-7.5)

scheduling.notes: Ruffled blue-green leaves. Flavor sweetens after frost. Harvest lower leaves first, leaving crown to grow. Very cold hardy.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in nitrogen (pH 6.0-7.5)

scheduling.notes: Also called dinosaur or Tuscan kale. Dark pebbled leaves; tender texture. More heat-tolerant than curly types.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in nitrogen (pH 6.5-7.5)
Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in nitrogen (pH 6.5-7.5)

scheduling.notes: Heirloom with crinkled (savoy) leaves. Bolt-resistant for a spinach. Sow early spring and fall. Harvest outer leaves first.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in nitrogen (pH 6.5-7.5)

scheduling.notes: Smooth-leaf hybrid. Excellent bolt resistance and good for baby leaf harvest. Fast-growing; ideal for succession sowing.

soilPreference: Loose, well-drained, sandy loam (pH 6.0-7.0)
Well-drained, deep loam free of rocks (pH 6.0-7.5)
Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in nitrogen (pH 5.8-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Plant in blocks of at least 4 rows for wind pollination. Harvest when silks are brown and dry. Needs rich soil and full sun.

soilPreference: Well-drained, loose, slightly acidic loam (pH 5.0-6.5)
Well-drained, loose, slightly acidic loam (pH 5.0-6.5)

scheduling.notes: Classic baking potato. Plant certified seed potatoes; hill soil as plants grow. Harvest after vines die back. Cure before storage.

soilPreference: Well-drained, loose, slightly acidic loam (pH 5.0-6.5)

scheduling.notes: Waxy texture; holds shape when cooked. Good for roasting and salads. Can harvest as new potatoes when plants flower.

soilPreference: Well-drained, loose, slightly acidic loam (pH 5.0-6.5)

scheduling.notes: Yellow flesh with buttery flavor. All-purpose; good boiled, mashed, or roasted. Medium storage life. Early to mid-season harvest.

soilPreference: Well-drained, loose, sandy loam (pH 5.8-6.2)
Well-drained, rich loam, slightly acidic (pH 5.5-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Indeterminate heirloom with exceptional flavor. Potato-leaf foliage. Prone to cracking; water evenly.

soilPreference: Well-drained, rich loam, slightly acidic (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Indeterminate orange cherry. Extremely sweet flavor. Vigorous grower; needs tall staking. Harvest when deep orange.

soilPreference: Well-drained, rich loam, slightly acidic (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Indeterminate Italian paste tomato. Meaty with few seeds; prized for sauce. Needs consistent moisture and staking.

soilPreference: Well-drained, rich loam, slightly acidic (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Harvest green for mild heat or red for more heat. Prolific producer. Corking (white lines) indicates maturity.

soilPreference: Well-drained, rich loam, slightly acidic (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Very hot. Requires long warm season; start seeds early. Harvest when orange for full heat and fruity flavor.

soilPreference: Well-drained, rich loam, slightly acidic (pH 6.0-6.8)

scheduling.notes: Mild sweet pepper. Harvest yellow-green or wait for red. Excellent for pickling and frying. Heavy yields.

soilPreference: Well-drained, moderately fertile loam (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Climbing variety; needs 6-8 ft support. Longer harvest window than bush types. Pick regularly to extend production.

soilPreference: Well-drained, moderately fertile loam (pH 6.0-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Needs warm soil (65F+) for germination. Harvest when pods are plump but still green. Both bush and pole types available.

soilPreference: Well-drained, moderately fertile loam (pH 6.0-7.5)

scheduling.notes: Flat edible pods harvested before peas swell. Shorter vines (3-4 ft). Cool season crop; production drops in heat.

soilPreference: Well-drained, fertile loam, rich in nitrogen (pH 5.8-7.0)

scheduling.notes: Let ears dry on stalk until husks are papery. Cure indoors for 4-6 weeks before shelling. Isolate from sweet corn varieties.
